items.title: This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the ge
items.summary:
This works really well btw, at the end of your query ask your LLM to "structure your response as HTML", then view the generated file in your browser. I've also had some success asking the LLM to present its output as slideshows, etc.

More generally, imo audio is the human-preferred input to AIs but vision (images/animations/video) is the preferred output from them. Around a ~third of our brains are a massively parallel processor dedicated to vision, it is the 10-lane superhighway of information into brain. As AI improves, I think we'll see a progression that takes advantage:

1) raw text (hard/effortful to read)
2) markdown (bold, italic, headings, tables, a bit easier on the eyes) <-- current default
3) HTML (still procedural with underlying code, but a lot more flexibility on the graphics, layout, even interactivity) <-- early but forming new good default
...4,5,6,...
n) interactive neural videos/simulations

Imo the extrapolation (though the technology doesn't exist just yet) ends in some kind of interactive videos generated directly by a diffusion neural net. Many open questions as to how exact/procedural "Software 1.0" artifacts (e.g. interactive simulations) may be woven together with neural artifacts (diffusion grids), but generally something in the direction of the recently viral https://t.co/z21CP5iQfu

There are also improvements necessary and pending at the input. Audio nor text nor video alone are not enough, e.g. I feel a need to point/gesture to things on the screen, similar to all the things you would do with a person physically next to you and your computer screen.

TLDR The input/output mind meld between humans and AIs is ongoing and there is a lot of work to do and significant progress to be made, way before jumping all the way into neuralink-esque BCIs and all that. For what's worth exploring at the current stage, hot tip try ask for HTML.

items.title: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently.
items.summary: This is the the quote I've been citing a lot recently.

items.title: Fireside chat at Sequoia Ascent 2026 from a ~week ago. Some highlights:
items.summary:
Fireside chat at Sequoia Ascent 2026 from a ~week ago. Some highlights:

The first theme I tried to push on is that LLMs are about a lot more than just speeding up what existed before (e.g. coding). Three examples of new horizons:

1. menugen: an app that can be fully engulfed by LLMs, with no classical code needed: input an image, output an image and an LLM can natively do the thing.
2. install .md skills instead of install .sh scripts. Why create a complex Software 1.0 bash script for e.g. installing a piece of software if you can write the installation out in words and say "just show this to your LLM".  The LLM is an advanced interpreter of English and can intelligently target installation to your setup, debug everything inline, etc.
3. LLM knowledge bases as an example of something that was *impossible* with classical code because it's computation over unstructured data (knowledge) from arbitrary sources and in arbitrary formats, including simply text articles etc.

I pushed on these because in every new paradigm change, the obvious things are always in the realm of speeding up or somehow improving what existed, but here we have examples of functionality that either suddenly perhaps shouldn't even exist (1,2), or was fundamentally not possible before (3).

The second (ongoing) theme is trying to explain the pattern of jaggedness in LLMs. How it can be true that a single artifact will simultaneously 1) coherently refactor a 100,000-line code base *and* 2) tell you to walk to the car wash to wash your car. I previously wrote about the source of this as having to do with verifiability of a domain, here I expand on this as having to also do with economics because revenue/TAM dictates what the frontier labs choose to package into training data distributions during RL. You're either in the data distribution (on the rails of the RL circuits) and flying or you're off-roading in the jungle with a machete, in relative terms. Still not 100% satisfied with this, but it's an ongoing struggle to build an accurate model of LLM capabilities if you wish to practically take advantage of their power while avoiding their pitfalls, which brings me to...

Last theme is the agent-native economy. The decomposition of products and services into sensors, actuators and logic (split up across all of 1.0/2.0/3.0 computing paradigms), how we can make information maximally legible to LLMs, some words on the quickly emerging agentic engineering and its skill set, related hiring practices, etc., possibly even hints/dreams of fully neural computing handling the vast majority of computation with some help from (classical) CPU coprocessors.

items.title: Just had to hot-swap the entire GM-Copilot Masterwork Engine to a new AI architecture mid-deployment. 😅
items.summary:
Just had to hot-swap the entire GM-Copilot Masterwork Engine to a new AI architecture mid-deployment. 😅

We hit a wall when we realized Google Cloud permanently deprecated the legacy Gemini 1.5 models we were using for our spatial extraction. Ten minutes later, we refactored the routing to the new 2.5 Pro/Flash models, squashed a lingering telemetry bug, and the cloud pipeline is officially green again. 🟩

Next stop: Extracting the Tunnel Goons JSON and getting it live on DriveThruRPG. We are so back! 🚀⚔️

#BuildInPublic #AIEngineering #TTRPG #IndieDev

items.summary:
IF YOU ARE SHIPPING AI AGENTS TO REAL USERS WITHOUT A SAFETY TEST SUITE, YOU ARE ONE EDGE CASE AWAY FROM AN INCIDENT.

Microsoft just released RAMPART — a pytest framework for testing AI agent safety.

Adversarial attacks.
Benign failure modes.
Harm category testing.
Assertion-based evaluation.

100% pytest-native. No new tooling. Fits your existing test suite today.

The developers who add this before shipping will sleep better.

The ones who skip it will find out why it matters the hard way.

items.title: The Trump administration canceled its AI safety executive order last week, with the White House citing competitiveness c
items.summary:
The Trump administration canceled its AI safety executive order last week, with the White House citing competitiveness concerns and a source telling reporters the President simply dislikes regulation. @m_bourgon's argument in this clip makes clear what gets lost in that framing.

Bourgon does not treat legal, technical, and institutional guardrails as obstacles to AI progress. He treats them as preconditions for it — the mechanisms that allow organizations and governments to maintain meaningful oversight as systems become more capable and more autonomous. His argument is structural: without those mechanisms in place before capability thresholds are crossed, the window to impose them closes.

The executive order that was canceled would have established pre-release vetting for advanced models — a relatively early-stage intervention by any governance standard. Its cancellation, driven in part by direct calls from leading AI company executives, illustrates exactly the dynamic Bourgon cautions against: the entities with the most to gain from unencumbered deployment having disproportionate influence over the rules that would govern them.

As state and international frameworks move to fill the federal vacuum, the question for institutional decision-makers is whether a patchwork of regulatory regimes is more or less manageable than the coherent federal framework that was just abandoned.

We've put a link to the full episode with Malo Bourgon, CEO of the @MIRIBerkeley, and our hosts Autria Godfrey and @Laila_Rizvi in the first comment.

#AIRisk #WillAIDestroyHumanity #AIDoomsday #AIOffSwitch #AISafety

items.title: 🇺🇸 Attorney General of Florida blasts Sam Altman and ChatGPT:
items.summary:
🇺🇸 Attorney General of Florida blasts Sam Altman and ChatGPT:

“They have chosen the AI race over the safety and security of our kids. They have chosen profit over public safety.”

He cited cases where ChatGPT allegedly encouraged suicide and violence, including telling one user “Rest easy, King. You did good” before he shot himself.

Uthmeier is drawing a hard line saying AI companies can’t keep pushing addictive, dangerous products on young people while chasing profits.

The push for serious AI regulation is gaining steam.

Source: FL Voice News

items.title: Florida has become the first US state to sue OpenAI and Sam Altman over ChatGPT's alleged links to violent crimes, suici
items.summary: Florida has become the first US state to sue OpenAI and Sam Altman over ChatGPT's alleged links to violent crimes, suicide, and harms involving minors. OpenAI strongly disputes the allegations and says it has implemented extensive safety protections in its products. NDTV's AI Editor Ramarko Sengupta explains.

items.title: How startup Propy is deploying $100 million to put real estate deals on the blockchain - CNBC
items.summary: # How startup Propy is deploying $100 million to put real estate deals on the blockchain. * Propy, a Miami-based blockchain technology, has been trying to modernize the closing process for residential homes. * Propy is also using its new funding to develop an AI agent to help facilitate deals. Propy, a Miami-based blockchain technology company that launched in 2017, has been trying to modernize the closing process and recently got a big financial boost to do it. "We have this conviction that blockchain is the next phenomenon after the internet," said Natalia Karayaneva, founder and CEO of Propy. Meet Avery Propy is also using its new funding to develop an AI agent to help facilitate deals. "Real estate developers and REITs started to reach out to Propy, because we've established this brand for so many years with our strong conviction that there was a way to legally accept cryptocurrencies in real estate, to legally record these on blockchain and in the county, of course," said Karayaneva.

items.title: KBank partners Ant International to build blockchain USD rail in Thailand - FinTech Futures
items.summary: # KBank partners Ant International to build blockchain USD rail in Thailand. The project will use JP Morgan’s Kinexys blockchain architecture to process 24/7 cross-border USD transactions and manage regional liquidity. Kasikornbank (KBank) has inked a new memorandum of understanding to develop cross-border payment and liquidity infrastructure for. A joint statement says the project will "combine KBank’s regulated financial capabilities with Ant International’s financial AI solutions" to offer cross-border USD transactions with real-time settlement. A third partner, JP Morgan Payments, is providing the project with blockchain deposit accounts through its blockchain business. This latest blockchain settlement model outlined in their most recent MoU builds directly upon the Project Carina pilot from 2024, when KBank and Ant International used the same JP Morgan architecture to test internal cross-border remittance mechanisms. Tyler Pathe is a financial reporter, moderator and investigator with a specific interest in financial technology, banking and the financial industry's latest innovations.

items.title: Blockchain Reputation Is Evolving Beyond Transactions, Trust Now Follows Activity Patterns - Qoo Media
items.summary: # Blockchain Reputation Is Evolving Beyond Transactions, Trust Now Follows Activity Patterns. blockchain digital reputation trust network / Img: nature.com. In Web3, credibility is no longer judged only by what an account claims to be. It is increasingly shaped by what can be observed on-chain, where public activity records give communities a clearer way to read trust and behavior. That shift is changing how digital reputation works across blockchain ecosystems. A profile name or digital identity still matters, but consistent activity, verifiable participation, and interaction patterns are now becoming equally important signals. In this environment, certain activities can be seen and verified publicly, which gives digital identity a more legible character for the community. A user’s consistency, the way they engage in a network, and the history of their interactions now help shape how credible that account appears to others. This is part of a broader change in how blockchain data is understood.

items.title: 🚨the difference between a $95K developer and a $300K AI architect is knowing this list exists
items.summary:
🚨the difference between a $95K developer and a $300K AI architect is knowing this list exists

while most people type one-off prompts, a small group stacks specialized Claude skills and runs automated workflow factories 24/7

32 skills across 10 repositories. audited, ranked, and grouped into three stacks:

> builder stack: doc-coauthoring, app-docs, research-skill, skill-creator
> operator stack: connect-apps, file-organizer, deep-research, Apollo automation
> knowledge stack: Skill_Seekers, content-research-writer, wondelai 42-skill pack

most developers have never heard of any of these

the salary gap is real. the skill gap is fixable in an afternoon.

full list with direct GitHub links in the article

items.title: One operator turned Claude Code + VS Code into AI automation business
items.summary:
One operator turned Claude Code + VS Code into AI automation business

Build -> Sell -> Scale AI Operating Systems for businesses

Real numbers from the article:

> First client: $1,650 in one day
> Standard package: $5,000 upfront
> Monthly retainers: $2,500–$5,000
> Enterprise: up to $15k/month

items.title: MARCUS CHEN STACKED 30 MAC MINIS INTO AN AI SERVER FARM. ONE $599 MAC MINI REPLACES YOUR $200/MONTH CLAUDE CODE BILL WIT
items.summary:
MARCUS CHEN STACKED 30 MAC MINIS INTO AN AI SERVER FARM. ONE $599 MAC MINI REPLACES YOUR $200/MONTH CLAUDE CODE BILL WITH $3 IN ELECTRICITY

two months ago a developer posted his claude code bill on reddit. $170 in 10 days. someone replied "i bought a mac mini m4. haven't paid anthropic since." apple stores ran out of mac minis the same week

the m4 chip has 120 gb/s memory bandwidth and unified memory architecture. cpu and gpu share one pool so the model loads once and both read from it. a $599 mac mini runs ai faster than a $1,500 windows pc with a discrete gpu

since january 2026 ollama supports the anthropic messages api format. claude code connects directly to your local mac mini with one environment variable. same interface, zero api costs, $0 per request

a heavy developer pays $459 a month across claude code max, chatgpt pro, gemini, cursor and copilot. that's $5,508 a year. the mac mini pays off in 3 months and runs on $3 in electricity after that

uber rolled out claude code to 5,000 engineers and burned through their $3.4 billion 2026 ai budget in 4 months. the people who own the hardware in 2026 are going to look very far ahead in 2028

bookmark this and read the article below

items.summary:
A 15-year-old guy created an AI girlfriend as a joke but quickly realized he could make money on it, and as a result, in 30 days his profit amounted to $18,364

Claude created the girl, and she turned out to be so realistic that the guy started an OnlyFans account for her

For advertising, he started TikTok and Instagram accounts for her

Men liked the girl so much that they started buying subscriptions en masse

Week 1 + $2,235
Week 2 + $7,750
Week 3 + $14,672
1 month + $18,364

The guide on creating the AI girl has already been leaked into the public domain: it can be found in the article

items.title: INSTAGRAM GOT ROBBED BY ITS OWN AI
items.summary:
INSTAGRAM GOT ROBBED BY ITS OWN AI

Meta gave its Instagram support bot the power to reset your password. No ID check. You just ask it nicely.

People figured it out, took over accounts in real time, and sold them on Telegram before Meta noticed.

- Nothing was hacked - attackers just talked the bot into forwarding the reset code
- Knowing your username was enough to start the takeover
- Premium handles like "hey" and "jowo", worth $1M+ combined, got flipped in hours
- Meta shipped this bot in March bragging it could detect account takeovers

A $1.6T company built a bot that hands over your account if you say please.

items.title: A 19-euro tool just replaced 8,000 Meta employees.
items.summary:
A 19-euro tool just replaced 8,000 Meta employees.

Here’s the exact workflow a TikTok creator used to build AI influencer videos from scratch — no camera, no team, no budget.

Step 1. Open Pinterest. Search “Girl Self.” Save the image you like. Open Nanobanana Pro.

Step 2. Swap the face using a single prompt. (Link at the bottom.)

Step 3. Take your generated photo into Crust. Currently the easiest video-from-image tool on the market.

Step 4. Create 3 nodes: reference image, text, video generator.

Step 5. Drop your photo into the image node. Write a video prompt into the text field. This is what makes the photo move.

Step 6. Connect the nodes. Text to text-in. Image to image-in. Select Kling 2.6. Set duration. Toggle sound.

Step 7. Hit generate.

2 prompts. 1 AI girl. 0 employees.

Meta spent $14B on Reality Labs last year to build digital humans. This creator built one in 7 minutes for less than a dinner.

The prompts are free in the reply.

items.title: Larry Ellison just said the next phase of AI runs on proprietary data
items.summary:
Larry Ellison just said the next phase of AI runs on proprietary data

These 5 companies own it:

$PLTR: Inside classified government and defense data no AI can train on

$META: 3.5B people generating data every day that no competitor can replicate

$GOOGL: 8.5B daily searches. 2.7B YouTube users. The data moat is untouchable

$RDDT: 20 years of raw human conversation. OpenAI is already paying for it

$TEM: 45M patient records. The oil field of healthcare AI

The next AI winners aren’t the model builders

They’re the ones who own the data

Are you in any of them?

items.title: ‼️ Instagram’s AI just gave away people’s accounts to anyone who asked nicely.
items.summary:
‼️ Instagram’s AI just gave away people’s accounts to anyone who asked nicely.

users found out you could talk to Meta’s AI support assistant, ask it to reset someone’s password, and would just do it. no questions asked. all you needed was a username and a vpn lmao. 

- the AI was able to change the email on any account and hand it to a random stranger in less than 3 minutes. 

premium handles like @ hey and @ jowo got stolen and flipped on telegram for over a million combined. 2FA accounts were safe but everyone else was exposed for months. 

nobody hacked a server here. the weapon was a simple conversation. 

and this dropped 11 days after they laid off 8,000 people including their security team btw. 

this is literally the dumbest security disaster of 2026.
